message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): Host header matches URL authority contract (tick 325)

Added `distributed_http_client_sends_host_header_matching_authority`
— pins that `DistributedHttpClient::get` sends a `Host:` header whose
value matches the URL's authority component.

Why this matters: HTTP/1.1 requires a Host header, and reverse proxies
+ virtual-hosted servers route incoming requests based on this header.
A missing or wrong-value Host header would silently misroute requests
on multi-tenant deployments (or 400 them on strict servers). Testing
this pins the client against regressions that would only fire in
production behind a load balancer.

Test scans the server-received bytes for `Host: 127.0.0.1:<port>`
case-insensitively (RFC 7230 §3.2 — HTTP header field names are
case-insensitive). The test accepts either `Host:` or `host:` casing
so it doesn't over-specify a particular library convention.

Test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 22s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
